Plastic (PET).notes: Einwegpfand (€0.25) applies to PET bottles since January 2025. Return at supermarket deposit machines.

prep: Place in the Gelber Sack / gelbe Tonne
Plastic.notes: Austria's yellow bin (Gelbe Tonne) accepts plastic packaging.

prep: Sort by color into the correct Altglascontainer
Glass.notes: Separate by color: white glass → weißer Behälter, colored glass → grüner/brauner Behälter.

prep: Place in the Gelbe Tonne or yellow bag
Aluminum.notes: Aluminum foil and trays also go into the yellow bin.

Cardboard.notes: Place in the blaue Tonne (blue bin) for paper/cardboard.

Paper.notes: Blaue Tonne accepts newspapers, books, and paper packaging.

prep: No plastic bags — use compostable bags or newspaper
Include vegetable peels, coffee grounds, eggshells
Organic.notes: Braune Tonne (brown bin) for organic waste. Meat and cooked food: check with Gemeinde.

prep: Do NOT place in household bins
Bring to a Mistplatz (recycling yard) or electronics retailer (Elektroaltgeräte-Rücknahme)
E-Waste.notes: Austrian law (ElAG 2005) requires free take-back at retailers selling similar products.

Mixed Waste.notes: Restmüll is incinerated for energy in Austria — reduce first.

prep: Place in a Altkleidersammlung (textile collection container)
Textile.notes: Many charity organizations and municipalities operate textile drop-offs.

Ceramic.notes: Ceramics and porcelain cannot be mixed with glass recycling.
